name: build-web-data-visualization
description: Design, critique, implement, test, and export web data visualizations in Codex, including charts, maps, dashboards, Gantt, UML, scrollytelling, reports, slides, mobile views, shareable state, and advanced WebGL concepts.

interface.shortDescription: Design, build, test, and export web data visualizations
interface.longDescription: Use Build Web Data Visualization to choose, design, implement, test, and export browser charts, dashboards, maps, geospatial scenes, WebGL/Three.js, Canvas, D3/SVG, Gantt charts, UML and software diagrams, scrollytelling, reports, PDFs, and slide decks. The plugin favors source-backed data, self-explanatory layouts, mobile and large-screen sibling designs, URL-shareable analysis state, accessible labels and fallbacks, semantic color roles, and visual effects tied to data or interaction state. For advanced page or visual-story work, it guides concept-first design with user-approved large-screen and mobile concepts, binding semantic design contracts, operational workspace shells, and implementation contracts for renderer ownership, coordinates, state, fallbacks, and QA.
defaultPrompt: Visually design this advanced visualization with large-screen and mobile concepts
Choose the best chart, layout, shareable state, grammar, and stack for this dataset
Design QA for this chart, dashboard, map, or WebGL visualization
